hero.description: We connect atomistic mechanisms with synthesis, characterization, and electrochemical behavior to create actionable materials-design rules.

areas.title: High-energy cathodes
areas.short: Rationally designing cathodes that combine high capacity, fast kinetics, and long-term structural stability.
areas.detail: We study Li-rich layered oxides, high-Ni cathodes, and disordered rocksalt materials. By connecting high-valent redox chemistry with structural evolution, we seek design rules for cathodes that sustain high energy density without sacrificing stability.

areas.title: Computational materials design
areas.short: Using first-principles calculations, high-throughput screening, and machine learning to accelerate discovery.
areas.detail: We evaluate synthesis feasibility, phase stability, ion diffusion, voltage, and defect chemistry at scales that are difficult to access experimentally. Large-scale computation helps us move from isolated intuition toward systematic materials discovery.

areas.title: Sodium-ion batteries
areas.short: Establishing chemistry-specific design strategies for affordable and sustainable sodium-ion energy storage.
areas.detail: The distinct size and chemistry of Na ions create structural behavior that cannot be treated as a simple extension of lithium-ion materials. We combine computation and experiment to discover electrodes with high energy, power capability, and long cycle life.

areas.title: Beyond lithium-ion
areas.short: Exploring solid ion conductors and next-generation chemistries beyond conventional lithium-ion systems.
areas.detail: Our broader program includes new ion conductors for all-solid-state batteries and emerging systems such as multivalent and lithium-air batteries. These projects extend our core approach: reveal fundamental mechanisms, then translate them into materials design.
